senses.definition: a person's ability to make good judgments and behave sensibly

examples.x: He may not have a college degree, but he's got plenty of horse sense.
In times of crisis, it's important to rely on your horse sense.
Common sense is just another term for horse sense.
She has a lot of horse sense when it comes to managing her finances.
Sometimes, all you need is a little horse sense to find a solution.

senses.definition: (of an argument, theory, etc.) to be believable or supported by evidence

examples.x: I'm not convinced by his explanation; it just doesn't hold water.
Her theory may sound good, but it doesn't hold water when you examine the evidence.
The proposal seems promising, but we need to see if it holds water in practice.
Your plan needs to be more comprehensive if it's going to hold water.
The detective's theory didn't hold water when all the facts were considered.

senses.definition: a person's ability to learn or understand something

examples.x: The professor's extensive knowledge is a result of years of exercising his gray matter.
We need to put our collective gray matter to work in order to find a solution to this challenge.
It's not just about memorization; you need to engage your gray matter to truly understand the subject.
The team's creative ideas demonstrate their exceptional gray matter.

senses.definition: natural or instinctive intelligence and common sense that comes from personal experience and intuition rather than formal education or training

examples.x: Her mother wit guided her through many challenging situations in life.
In his grandmother's presence, you could always count on a dose of mother wit and sage advice.
It's not just book smarts that matter; her mother wit helps her make wise decisions.
His mother wit told him not to invest in that risky business venture.
She relied on her mother wit to navigate the complexities of the corporate world.

senses.definition: used when one does something even though one knows it is not sensible

examples.x: She agreed to lend him money against her better judgment because he was in a tough spot.
He took the job offer in another city against his better judgment because it offered a higher salary.
Against my better judgment, I decided to try that new restaurant even though the reviews were mixed.
They entered into the risky partnership against their better judgment, hoping for a big payoff.
She chose to confront the issue head-on against her better judgment, believing it was the right thing to do.

senses.definition: to speak in a clear and reasonable manner
the mental state of actively engaging in thought or problem-solving, often with an emphasis on creativity or generating ideas

examples.x: Time to put on your thinking cap and brainstorm some innovative solutions.
I'll need to bring my thinking cap to figure out this puzzle.
She always puts on her thinking cap during our team meetings, and her ideas are consistently brilliant.
Don't forget to bring your thinking cap to the brainstorming session; we need fresh ideas.
The team pull on their thinking caps to analyze market trends and develop a forward-thinking business plan for the upcoming year.

senses.definition: to start thinking or acting in a way that seems foolish

examples.x: I can't believe he invested all his savings in that risky scheme; he must have taken leave of his senses.
When she started yelling at her boss during the meeting, it was clear she had taken leave of her senses.
His decision to quit his stable job without another offer lined up seems like he's taken leave of his senses.
I don't know what got into her when she said those outrageous things; it's like she took leave of her senses.

word_list.word: (an old|a wise) head on young shoulders
word_list.pronunciation: ɐn ˈoʊld ɐ wˈaɪz hˈɛd ˌɑːn jˈʌŋ ʃˈoʊldɚz

senses.definition: a young person or a child who talks and behaves like an older or more experienced person would

examples.x: Sarah may be only 20, but she handles her responsibilities at work with the maturity of a wise head on young shoulders.
It's impressive to see how he manages his finances at 25; he truly has an old head on young shoulders.
Despite being the youngest member of the team, Mark's leadership and decision-making reflect an old head on young shoulders
Jenny's ability to provide wise advice to her friends demonstrates that she has a wise head on young shoulders.
His calm and composed demeanor during the crisis showed that he possesses an old head on young shoulders.

senses.definition: exceptionally wise compared to most people at one's age
